summary: This week in AI news, major developments include Meta removing a controversial AI feature from Instagram, Apple suing OpenAI for alleged trade secret theft, and OpenAI launching its new family of models with GPT-5.6. Open source AI continues to grow, with Hugging Face's CEO emphasizing its importance. Regulatory actions are also on the rise, with the EU warning Meta about autoplay and infinite scroll.
insight: The increasing regulatory scrutiny of AI technologies highlights the need for companies to prioritize transparency and accountability in their development and deployment of AI models.
highlights: Meta removes AI feature from Instagram after backlash
OpenAI launches new family of models with GPT-5.6
Apple sues OpenAI for alleged trade secret theft

articles.description: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

articles.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.
